tools.title: PDF Text Editor
tools.intent: Overlay or replace visible text on PDF pages in your browser—no cloud upload.
tools.description: Add or overlay text on PDF pages locally in your browser. Place text layers with optional white cover behind edits—your document never leaves your device.
useCases: Fix a typo or date on a form without re-exporting from Word
Add a note or reference number to an official PDF
Overlay corrected labels on scanned PDF packets

faq.q: Is my PDF uploaded to JoinMyPDF?
faq.a: No. Text editing runs entirely in your browser with pdf-lib. Your file never leaves your device.

faq.q: Can I edit existing text inside the PDF?
faq.a: This tool overlays new text layers and can cover underlying content with a white box. It does not rewrite native PDF text objects—use PDF to Word for deep text edits.

faq.q: Will the layout change?
faq.a: Original page dimensions and structure stay the same. Only the text layers you place are added on top.

faq.q: Can I edit password-protected PDFs?
faq.a: Yes. Enter the document password, place your text layers, then download the edited file.

tools.title: Compare Two PDFs
tools.primaryKeyword: compare two pdfs online side by side
tools.intent: Upload two PDFs and see text and layout differences highlighted side by side—100% in your browser.
tools.description: Compare two PDF files online with a side-by-side viewer. Highlights added text in green, removed text in red, and moved blocks in amber—processed locally with pdf.js, no uploads.
useCases: Review contract redlines before signing
Spot changes between policy or SOW versions
Verify that a revised PDF matches the approved draft

faq.q: Are my PDFs uploaded to JoinMyPDF?
faq.a: No. Both files are read and compared entirely in your browser using pdf.js.

faq.q: What kinds of changes can the tool detect?
faq.a: It compares extractable text per page—additions, deletions, and blocks that moved position. Scanned image-only PDFs without text layers may show no highlights.

faq.q: Can I compare PDFs with different page counts?
faq.a: Yes. Extra pages are treated as all-new or all-removed content on that side.

faq.q: Does this replace legal redlining software?
faq.a: It is a fast privacy-first check in the browser. For formal track-changes workflows, export from Word or your CLM and use this to verify the final PDF.

documentation.whyItMatters: Upload two PDFs and see text and layout differences highlighted side by side—100% in your browser.

tools.title: PDF to Booklet (Imposition)
tools.intent: Arrange PDF pages for saddle-stitch booklet printing with a live sheet preview—100% in your browser.
tools.description: Turn a PDF into a print-ready booklet with correct page imposition. Choose paper size (Letter, A4, or custom inches/cm), preview each folded sheet, and download locally—no uploads.
useCases: Print event programs or zines at home on a duplex printer
Impose training manuals for fold-and-staple distribution
Prepare church bulletins or club newsletters as booklets

faq.q: Are files uploaded to JoinMyPDF?
faq.a: No. Imposition runs locally in your browser with pdf-lib.

faq.q: How should I print the output PDF?
faq.a: Use duplex printing. For portrait booklets on Letter/A4, choose flip on the long edge unless your printer guide says otherwise.

faq.q: What if my page count is not a multiple of four?
faq.a: The tool pads with blank pages at the end so folding order stays correct.

faq.q: Does this work for perfect-bound books?
faq.a: This tool targets saddle-stitch (folded booklet) imposition. Perfect binding needs a different layout.

faq.a: This tool rasterizes each PDF page to a high-quality PNG inside the XPS package—visual fidelity matches your source, but text is not re-extracted as selectable XPS glyphs. For editable text, use PDF to Word; for reflowable reading, try PDF to EPUB.

faq.q: Can I open the XPS file on Mac or Linux?

documentation.whyItMatters: Sometimes you only need the sound: a lecture recording, a podcast exported as MP4, a music bed trapped in a screen capture. A free online video to MP3 converter that runs in the browser extracts that track without uploading the file. FFmpeg’s `-vn` flag ignores video; `libmp3lame` with `-q:a 2` builds a high-quality VBR MP3 that sounds excellent for speech and music. This guide covers how to extract audio from video online, how MP3 compares to AAC, and why local-first extraction is faster and safer than cloud converters. Treat extraction as a deliberate publishing step: keep the video master for archives, ship the MP3 for listening, and never confuse the two when you measure “file size saved.” If a clip is long, trim to the useful segment first so you are not encoding minutes of silence or unused intros into the MP3.

faq.q: What is the difference between PDF and PDF/A?
faq.a: Standard PDF is a flexible delivery format—fonts, colors, and interactive layers can vary between viewers. PDF/A is an ISO archival subset designed for long-term preservation: it restricts features that break over decades (like reliance on external content) and embeds metadata that records how the file should be interpreted in the future.

faq.q: Is this conversion secure?
faq.a: Yes. The entire workflow runs locally in your browser. Your contracts, filings, and institutional records are never uploaded to JoinMyPDF servers—ideal for privileged legal material and sensitive government documents.

faq.q: Does this support batch conversion?
faq.a: This release converts one PDF at a time so you can verify output before submission. For multi-file archival batches, convert each document individually, then merge with our PDF Merge tool if your repository accepts a single deliverable.

faq.q: Will my PDF/A file pass every validator?
faq.a: JoinMyPDF applies archival-oriented normalization—flattening interactive forms, rebuilding a clean structure, disabling object streams, and embedding PDF/A identification metadata. Complex source files (missing font embedding, transparency-heavy artwork, or scanned-only pages) may still require institutional preflight with tools like veraPDF before final deposit.

faq.q: Which PDF/A profile should I choose?
faq.a: PDF/A-1b is the most widely requested baseline for digital archives and many court e-filing portals. PDF/A-2b supports newer PDF features such as JPEG 2000 compression—choose it when your receiving authority explicitly allows PDF/A-2.

tools.title: PDF Linearization (Fast Web View)
tools.primaryKeyword: pdf linearization fast web view online
tools.intent: Convert PDFs to linearized, web-optimized files so browsers can display the first pages while the rest downloads—ideal for manuals, reports, and embedded viewers.
tools.description: Linearize PDF for Fast Web View—rebuild structure for progressive browser loading, enable web-optimized delivery, and download locally. 100% client-side processing with no uploads.
useCases: Publish product manuals and white papers on your website so readers see page one instantly
Prepare large annual reports for in-browser viewing without forcing a full download first
Optimize PDFs embedded in customer portals and knowledge bases for faster perceived load times

faq.q: What is PDF Linearization?
faq.a: Linearization (also called Fast Web View) reorganizes a PDF so the first page and supporting objects appear at the start of the file. Web browsers and PDF.js viewers can then render early pages while the remainder of the document streams in—instead of waiting for the entire file to download.

faq.q: Does this affect file quality?
faq.a: No. Linearization changes how the PDF is structured on disk, not how pages look. Text, images, vectors, and fonts are copied as-is—there is no recompression or downsampling.

faq.q: Why is it better for web viewing?
faq.a: Large PDFs on slow connections feel instant when linearized: users read the cover and table of contents while later chapters download in the background. That improves bounce rates, accessibility on mobile networks, and satisfaction in embedded document viewers.

faq.q: Is this tool private and local?
faq.a: Yes. Your PDF is processed entirely in your browser with WebAssembly and JavaScript. Nothing is uploaded to JoinMyPDF servers—ideal for confidential reports and internal documentation.

faq.q: How does JoinMyPDF linearize a PDF?
faq.a: The tool rebuilds a clean PDF with pages in order, disables object streams for broad viewer compatibility, and sets the catalog Linearized flag. For mission-critical publishing pipelines that require full hint-table linearization, validate output with your CMS or a dedicated preflight tool.

documentation.whyItMatters: Convert PDFs to linearized, web-optimized files so browsers can display the first pages while the rest downloads—ideal for manuals, reports, and embedded viewers.

faq.q: How does N-Up printing work?
faq.a: N-Up imposition places multiple source pages on each output sheet in a grid—for example, four pages in a 2×2 layout. Each page is scaled to fit its cell while keeping aspect ratio, so you print more content per physical sheet.

tools.title: PDF Signature Validator
tools.intent: Analyze a PDF for PKCS#7 digital signatures, verify document integrity after signing, and review signer metadata—without modifying the file.
tools.description: Validate PDF digital signatures online. Detect cryptographic signatures, check whether the document was altered after signing, and view signer name and signing date—100% local-first with no uploads.
useCases: Confirm a vendor contract was not tampered with before approval
Audit compliance packets for valid PKCS#7 signatures before archival
Verify government or banking PDFs received by email before processing

faq.q: What does signature validation mean?
faq.a: Validation checks whether a PDF contains a PKCS#7 digital signature and whether the signed byte ranges still match the cryptographic hash. If content was changed after signing, validation fails—indicating the document may have been altered.

faq.q: Is my document data secure?
faq.a: Yes. The validator reads your PDF entirely in your browser. Files are never uploaded to JoinMyPDF servers, and the tool does not modify your document.

faq.q: Can it detect if the document was altered?
faq.a: Yes. For standard Adobe PKCS#7 detached signatures, JoinMyPDF recomputes the hash over the signed byte ranges and verifies it against the embedded signature. A mismatch means the file changed after signing.

faq.q: Does this validate certificate authority trust?
faq.a: This tool verifies cryptographic integrity and parses signer certificate metadata. Full chain-of-trust validation (revocation, CA trust stores) may require enterprise PDF viewers such as Adobe Acrobat.

faq.q: Will visual signatures from Sign PDF be detected?
faq.a: No. Drawn or typed visual signatures are not PKCS#7 digital signatures. Use this tool for cryptographically signed PDFs from Adobe Sign, DocuSign exports, or certificate-based signing workflows.

documentation.whyItMatters: Color is one of the most reused tokens in modern product design. A single brand blue might appear as a hex code in a style guide, an rgb() value in a CSS custom property, and an hsl() triplet in a theme engine that generates lighter and darker variants. Understanding how HEX, RGB, and HSL differ—and when each format shines—helps designers and developers keep systems consistent, accessible, and easy to maintain. Teams that treat color as a shared contract between design and engineering ship fewer “almost right” buttons and fewer dark-mode surprises. This guide explains the three color spaces, their web-development use cases, how rounding and conversion can introduce tiny drifts, and how a local online color converter fits into everyday UI work without uploading confidential brand palettes to a third-party server.

faq.q: Why do websites need User-Agent information?

faq.q: Can I spoof my User-Agent?
faq.a: Yes. DevTools, extensions, and some browsers let you override the UA string. This tool accepts any pasted string so you can see how a spoofed or custom UA would be classified—spoofing does not hide other signals like IP or TLS fingerprinting.

faq.a: Browser name and version, operating system name and version, device type (Desktop, Mobile, Tablet, or Bot/Crawler), and rendering engine (for example Blink, WebKit, or Gecko).

faq.q: Can I copy the parsed report?
faq.a: Yes. Use Copy JSON to export the structured result to your clipboard.

Confirm a signature segment exists without verifying cryptography
Spot expired exp claims before debugging auth flows

faq.q: What is a JWT?
faq.a: A JSON Web Token is a compact, URL-safe string with three Base64Url parts — Header, Payload, and Signature — commonly used for API auth and session claims.

faq.q: Is my token secure on JoinMyPDF?
faq.a: Decoding runs entirely in your browser. Your JWT is never uploaded to JoinMyPDF. Still treat live production tokens carefully: anyone with the string can read its payload claims.

faq.q: Why doesn't this verify the signature?
faq.a: Cryptographic verification needs your HMAC secret or public key. This tool deliberately omits that so tokens stay private and decode-only. It only reports whether the signature segment is present, missing, malformed, or alg=none.

faq.q: Can I copy the payload JSON?
faq.a: Yes. Use Copy JSON on the payload panel to copy pretty-printed claims to your clipboard.

faq.q: How is expiration shown?
faq.a: If the payload includes exp, the tool shows a human-readable relative time plus the absolute local date, and flags expired tokens.

faq.q: Is the JWT Decoder free?
faq.a: Yes. It is free to use with no account required.

faq.q: Why are there different storage sizes for the same file?
faq.a: Vendors often advertise capacities using decimal (SI) units where 1 GB = 1,000³ bytes, while many operating systems report binary (IEC) units where 1 GiB = 1,024³ bytes. The same byte count therefore looks smaller when shown as GiB than as GB.

faq.q: What is the maximum supported unit?
faq.a: Petabyte (PB, decimal) and pebibyte (PiB, binary). You can also convert from or into Bytes, KB/KiB, MB/MiB, GB/GiB, and TB/TiB.

faq.a: KB uses a base of 1,000 (1 KB = 1,000 bytes). KiB uses a base of 1,024 (1 KiB = 1,024 bytes). Choose SI labels for decimal marketing sizes and IEC labels for binary OS/developer sizes.

faq.q: Is my data uploaded?

faq.q: What are the supported bases?

faq.a: Digits must be valid for the selected From base. Binary only allows 0 and 1; octal allows 0–7; hex allows 0–9 and A–F.

documentation.whyItMatters: Placeholder text is one of the oldest tricks in visual communication—and still one of the most useful. Designers need mass without meaning so layouts can be judged on hierarchy, spacing, and rhythm. Developers need content that stretches components the way real copy will. Writers need to see how a column behaves before the final article lands. Lorem Ipsum became the default filler because it looks like Latin prose without distracting readers with readable English. This guide covers the history of Lorem Ipsum, how to use placeholder text well in design and development, and practical tips for testing responsive typography. Pair it with the Free Online Lorem Ipsum Generator above when you need quick, local, realistically structured dummy copy. tools.intent: Show your public IP address instantly, plus ISP, location, connection type, and VPN/proxy hints — with interactive lookup for any IP.
tools.description: Public IP address tool: check my IP location, ISP, and connection details on load, then look up another IP online. Data is fetched in your browser; JoinMyPDF does not store or log your IP.
useCases: Confirm the public IP a remote API sees from your network
Verify VPN connectivity by comparing expected vs observed location
Look up another IP for ISP and geo hints
Copy User-Agent and OS details while testing device-specific bugs

faq.q: How is my location determined?
faq.a: Approximate city, region, and country come from an IP geolocation database maintained by the lookup provider your browser contacts (such as ipapi.co). It is not GPS and can be wrong for VPNs, CGNAT, or mobile networks.

faq.q: Is my IP being tracked?
faq.a: JoinMyPDF does not store or log your IP for this tool. The request goes from your browser to a public IP API. We do not receive or keep the result on our servers.

faq.q: What is the difference between IPv4 and IPv6?
faq.a: IPv4 uses 32-bit dotted addresses (like 203.0.113.10). IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses written with colons. When dual-stack is detectable, this tool may show a companion address alongside your primary public IP.

faq.q: Can I look up someone else's IP?
faq.a: Yes. Use Look up another IP with a public IPv4 or IPv6 address to fetch ISP, location, and connection hints. Only public metadata is shown.

faq.q: Does this detect VPN or proxy?
faq.a: When the provider reports security flags (VPN, proxy, Tor, or hosting), they appear under VPN / Proxy and in the connection type summary. Flags are informational and not guaranteed to be complete.

faq.q: Is the IP lookup free?
faq.a: Yes. It is free to use with no account required.
